overview.description: Add default post-install hooks to tools that would benefit from automatic shell integration, PATH configuration, or completion setup. These hooks should be idempotent, advisory (non-fatal on failure), and overridable by user-defined hooks.
goals: Identify tools that need shell integration
Add completion script installation for CLI tools
Add PATH configuration for tools installed outside standard paths
Ensure hooks are idempotent (safe to run multiple times)

userStories.title: Shell Completion Hooks
userStories.description: As a developer, I want CLI tools to automatically install shell completions so that tab completion works out of the box.

acceptanceCriteria.criterion: Add completion hook for just (just --completions bash/zsh)
Add completion hook for flux (flux completion bash/zsh)
Add completion hook for argocd (argocd completion bash/zsh)
Add completion hook for eksctl (eksctl completion bash/zsh)
Add completion hook for glab (glab completion -s bash/zsh)

userStories.title: PATH Configuration Hooks
userStories.description: As a developer, I want tools that install outside standard PATH to automatically configure shell PATH so that commands work immediately.

acceptanceCriteria.criterion: Identify tools that install outside standard PATH
Add PATH export to shell config files (.bashrc, .zshrc)
Ensure PATH additions are idempotent (check before adding)

userStories.title: Shell Integration Hooks
userStories.description: As a developer, I want tools with shell integration features to automatically configure them so that they work after installation.
candidateTools: fd (often aliased or paired with fzf)

acceptanceCriteria.criterion: Add delta hook to configure as git pager
Verify bat already has hook (yes - bat-extras setup)
Document tools that don't need hooks

existingHooks.description: Tools that already have default hooks implemented

toolsWithoutHooks.description: Tools that currently do not have default hooks
noHookNeeded: jq - Pure CLI tool, no shell integration
curl - Pure CLI tool, no shell integration
htop - Terminal UI, no shell integration
btop - Terminal UI, no shell integration
age - Encryption tool, no shell integration
aria2 - Download manager, no shell integration
cue - Config language, no shell integration

hookPattern.template:
default_hook: {
    description: "Description of what the hook does",
    script: r#"
# Add to .zshrc if not present
if [ -f "$HOME/.zshrc" ] && ! grep -q 'pattern' "$HOME/.zshrc"; then
    echo 'command' >> "$HOME/.zshrc"
fi
"#
}

functionalRequirements.description: All hooks must be idempotent (safe to run multiple times)
Hooks must check if configuration already exists before modifying files
Hooks are advisory - failures should warn but not fail installation
User-defined hooks in jarvy.toml override default hooks

technicalConsiderations.details: Hooks should detect user's shell and configure appropriately (.bashrc vs .zshrc vs .config/fish)
Use grep to check if configuration already exists before appending
Some hooks may need platform-specific variants (macOS vs Linux paths)

risks.mitigation: Always check before appending, use safe grep patterns
Test hooks multiple times, verify no duplicate entries
